latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
Today 14:30
RNS Number : 5045Z
Videndum PLC
07 April 2026
7 April 2026
Videndum plc
Board Changes
Videndum plc ("Videndum" or "the Company") announces that Martin Cooke will cease to be an independent non-executive director of the Company with effect from 30 April 2026. Martin was appointed at the end of July 2025 to support the Board with the refinancing of the business that successfully completed on 30 March 2026. At the same time, Graham Oldroyd will cease to be Deputy Chairman and will revert to his former role as Chair of the Finance Committee and an independent non-executive director.
Commenting on the Board changes, Stephen Harris, Videndum's Chairman said: "On behalf of the Board and our stakeholders I would like to express thanks to Martin who has provided invaluable support to enable Videndum to refinance its business and to secure its future in the most challenging of circumstances. Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the Board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure. Graham will revert to his previous role as an independent non-executive director and Chair of the Finance Committee. We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent Chief Executive Officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to."
For more information please contact:
Videndum plc
Stephen Harris, Chairman Email:
IR-enquiries@videndum.com
Brian Morgan, Group Chief Financial Officer
FTI Consulting
Richard Mountain
Telephone: 020 3272 1340
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the content creation market.
Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, bags, backgrounds, audio capture, and noise reduction equipment.
We employ around 1,200 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/
LEI number: 2138007H5DQ4X8YOCF14

company_30s.what_they_do: VID.L is a small‑cap video‑technology firm that creates and distributes niche video content through a proprietary streaming platform. It also offers production tools for independent creators, allowing them to monetize short‑form and long‑form videos.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The video‑streaming market is growing at double‑digit rates, and VID’s focus on underserved genres gives it a defensible niche. Its production tools can attract a large creator community, which fuels user growth and advertising revenue. If the company can scale its platform, it could tap a multi‑billion‑pound market that is still under‑served by the big players.
company_30s.current_state: VID is currently in a cash‑burn phase, having just completed a capital raise and refinancing package. The company is still pre‑revenue, relying on investor funding to build out its platform and secure content deals.

rns_narrative: Financial reset: The company has just posted its full‑year results and then moved quickly to raise capital and refinance its debt, signalling a push to shore up liquidity.
Governance shuffle: Several director declarations and a formal meeting of shareholders suggest a realignment of leadership and strategy.
Strategic positioning: The open offer and prospectus filings hint at an upcoming product launch or content partnership that could unlock value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bleeding but hopeful – the community sees the dilution pain but still looks for a turnaround.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the fresh capital as proof that smart money believes VID can turn its platform around.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most voices warn that the dilution is too steep and the company has no clear revenue path, fearing another slide.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Early chatter was overwhelmingly negative, but the capital‑raise announcements have injected a faint optimism that the stock might start a recovery.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat and dead – a perfect stage for a surprise catalyst to move the price.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the open offer – if the company secures the expected share price, it will have the cash to invest in platform development.
Refinancing finalised – a lower debt burden could improve the balance sheet and signal stability.
Announcement of a new content partnership or product launch – any deal that expands user base or revenue streams would be a clear trigger.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 3‑month average, a price bounce to the 200‑day moving average, or a press release detailing new contracts or revenue projections.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates tightening around a potential support level, but the stock is still in the early, high‑volatility phase of a recovery. Confirmation will come when the price breaks above the recent low and volume confirms the move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: VID is trading at a deep discount after a severe sell‑off. The recent capital raise shows that institutional buyers are willing to inject cash, which is a strong sign of confidence. If the price rebounds to the midpoint of its pre‑crash range, the upside is roughly 3‑5× the current value, while the downside is capped at the current price level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s dilution is heavy, and there is no clear path to revenue yet. If the platform fails to attract users or secure profitable content deals, the price could fall further into the lower tail of the 52‑week range.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside 3–5×, potential downside limited to the present price. The asymmetry is favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 200‑day moving average with a volume increase of at least 1.5× the 3‑month average. Allocate 10% of the portfolio to the position and set a stop 30% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a clear catalyst – such as a partnership announcement or a successful open‑offer result – confirms that the capital raise will be used productively.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the only terminal risk is continued dilution, which is already priced in.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VID’s deep discount after a heavy dilution, coupled with a fresh capital injection, creates a high‑reward, low‑downside play that could ignite a sharp rebound if the market believes the platform can deliver.
